title: Thelypteris elmerorum
intro: Thelypteris elmerorum, also known as the Elmer's maidenhair fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Thelypteris elmerorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Thelypteris elmerorum is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory conditions.
taxonomy: Thelypteris elmerorum is classified in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the order Polypodiales. This species is further classified within the genus Thelypteris. Thelypteris elmerorum is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. Its classification is also associated with the subclass Polypodiidae.
